fields.Text:
Back pain, asymmetric leg weakness, sensory deficits, and bowel/bladder loss. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Cauda equina syndrome}}
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Cauda equina syndrome is mostly caused by herniated intervertebral discs compressing the cauda equina nerve roots, usually at L4/5 or L5/S1 levels. This is a neurosurgical emergency and requires prompt diagnosis and treatment. Permanent damage and loss of function can result from delays in treatment.




Open review Q1

fields.Text: Cauda equina syndrome is a {{c1::neurosurgical emergency}} usually caused by L4/5 or L5/S1 disc herniation compressing cauda equina roots.
fields.Extra:

This is a neurosurgical emergency and requires prompt diagnosis and treatment. Permanent damage and loss of function can result from delays in treatment.





Open review Q1

fields.Text:
Osteochondroma. Most common vascular complication?

{{c1::Pseudoaneurysm}}
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Pseudoaneurysm is the most common vascular complication of osteochondroma, predominantly affecting the femoropopliteal segment but also rarely involving the brachial artery in the upper extremity.




Open review Q2

fields.Text: The most common vascular complication of osteochondroma is {{c1::pseudoaneurysm}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Pseudoaneurysm is the most common vascular complication of osteochondroma, predominantly affecting the femoropopliteal segment but also rarely involving the brachial artery in the upper extremity.

fields.Text:
Distal phalanx Seymour fracture with nail-bed bleeding. Specific concern?

{{c1::Osteomyelitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q3. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Seymour fracture is an open Salter-Harris fracture of the distal phalanx with nail bed injury, creating communication between fracture and external environment with high risk of osteomyelitis requiring prompt surgical irrigation and antibiotics.




Open review Q3

fields.Text: Seymour fracture is an open Salter-Harris distal phalanx fracture with nail-bed injury and high risk of {{c1::osteomyelitis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Seymour fracture is an open Salter-Harris fracture of the distal phalanx with nail bed injury, creating communication between fracture and external environment with high risk of osteomyelitis requiring prompt surgical irrigation and antibiotics.

fields.Text: A Seymour fracture is an open distal phalanx physeal fracture with nail-bed injury, so the specific concern is {{c1::osteomyelitis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Seymour fracture is an open Salter-Harris fracture of the distal phalanx with nail bed injury, creating communication between fracture and external environment with high risk of osteomyelitis requiring prompt surgical irrigation and antibiotics.

fields.Text: Hypertrophic osteoarthropathy in a smoker should prompt {{c1::chest CT}} to evaluate for lung cancer.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hypertrophic osteoarthropathy presents as symmetric "railroad-track" cortical uptake on bone scan with diffuse periostitis. In smokers, this strongly suggests underlying lung cancer (most commonly adenocarcinoma) requiring urgent chest imaging for primary tumor detection and staging.




Open review Q4

fields.Text:
Breast US mass. Malignant orientation feature?

{{c1::Not parallel/taller-than-wide}}
fields.Extra:
Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Malignant U/S features include spiculation, not parallel orientation, angular margins, micro-calcifications, and posterior acoustic shadowing.





Open review Q5

fields.Text: Suspicious breast US features include {{c1::not-parallel orientation}}, spiculation, angular margins, microcalcifications, and shadowing.
fields.Extra:
Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Malignant U/S features include spiculation, not parallel orientation, angular margins, micro-calcifications, and posterior acoustic shadowing.






Open review Q5

fields.Text:
Post-gastric band CT with partial intraluminal band migration. Complication?

{{c1::Intragastric erosion}}
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Intragastric erosion of the gastric band is a delayed complication that possibly results from increased pressure against the gastric band resulting in necrosis and partial intraluminal migration. Given the risks of perforation, bowel obstruction and GI bleed, bands that have eroded into the gastric lumen are surgically removed.




Open review Q6

fields.Text: Intragastric erosion of gastric band is treated with {{c1::surgical removal}} because of perforation, obstruction, and bleeding risk.
fields.Extra:

Open review Q6

fields.Text:
Expanded T1-hyperintense frontal sinus mucocele. Signal due to?

{{c1::Proteinaceous contents}}
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The non-contrast T1-weighted magnetic resonance image (MRI) shows a hyperintense opacified, expanded left frontal sinus (red arrow) with smooth bony remodeling of walls (blue arrows), which, along with the clinical symptoms, is suggestive of sinonasal mucocele.





Open review Q7

fields.Text: Sinonasal mucocele contents can be T1 hyperintense from {{c1::proteinaceous material}}.

fields.Text: Radiation sentinel events are now {{c1::outcome-based rather than dose-threshold-based}}.
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The sentinel event threshold changed from dose-based (15 Gy PSD) to outcome-based (permanent tissue injury with substandard care), recognizing that high doses alone don't indicate poor practice, while tissue reactions can occur at lower doses when proper protocols aren't followed.




Open review Q8

fields.Text:
Parent-daughter radionuclide graph where daughter half-life is shorter. Equilibrium type?

{{c1::Transient equilibrium}}
fields.Extra:
Q9. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Transient equilibrium is a state in which the daughter radionuclide half-life is shorter than that of the parent radionuclide.





Open review Q9

fields.Text: Transient equilibrium occurs when the daughter radionuclide half-life is {{c1::shorter than the parent half-life}}.
fields.Extra:

fields.Text: In women under 30 with a palpable breast mass, initial imaging is {{c1::targeted ultrasound}}.
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Targeted ultrasound to the palpable finding is the appropriate initial imaging for women under 30 due to low cancer incidence and dense breast tissue limiting mammographic sensitivity.




Open review Q10

fields.Text:
Peptic ulcer disease without NSAID use. Most likely cause?

{{c1::Helicobacter pylori infection}}
fields.Extra:
Q11. Correct; difficulty: hard. The principal cause of peptic ulcer disease is Helicobacter pylori infection, which affects approximately 42% of patients with peptic ulcer disease, and aspirin or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) use, which are etiologic factors in approximately 36% of people with peptic ulcer disease.





Open review Q11

fields.Text:
Pneumobilia, small bowel obstruction, and ectopic gallstone. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Gallstone ileus}}
fields.Extra:
Q12. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Rigler's triad of pneumobilia, small bowel obstruction, and gallstone are characteristic of gallstone ileus.

fields.Text:
Rapidly growing painless breast mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Phyllodes tumor}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Phyllodes tumors are uncommon fibroepithelial breast tumors that are capable of a diverse range of biologic behaviors.




Open review Q13

fields.Text: Phyllodes tumor is a {{c1::fibroepithelial breast tumor}} that often presents as a rapidly growing painless mass.
fields.Extra:
Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Phyllodes tumors are uncommon fibroepithelial breast tumors that are capable of a diverse range of biologic behaviors.

fields.Text: Contrast anaphylaxis with wheezing, urticaria, hypotension, and tachycardia is treated first with {{c1::0.3 mg IM epinephrine}}.
fields.Extra:
Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Intramuscular epinephrine is the best initial treatment route for a patient who develops wheezing, urticaria, hypotension, and tachycardia after a CT scan with contrast. Intramuscular administration in the mid-outer thigh is preferred for rapid absorption and safety. Intravenous epinephrine is reserved for refractory cases due to higher risk of adverse events and dosing errors.




Open review Q14

fields.Text:
Young woman with classic fibroadenoma imaging features. Diagnosis/management?

{{c1::Fibroadenoma; short-term follow-up}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Classic fibroadenoma appears as oval, circumscribed, isodense mass on mammography and homogeneous hypoechoic mass with circumscribed margins on ultrasound, as seen here. When imaging features are typical, conservative management with imaging surveillance up to 2 years is appropriate (BI-RADS 3), provided growth remains <20% in any 6-month period.




Open review Q15

fields.Text: Classic fibroadenoma may be managed with {{c1::short-term imaging follow-up}} when typical and stable.
fields.Extra:
Q15. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Classic fibroadenoma appears as oval, circumscribed, isodense mass on mammography and homogeneous hypoechoic mass with circumscribed margins on ultrasound, as seen here. When imaging features are typical, conservative management with imaging surveillance up to 2 years is appropriate (BI-RADS 3), provided growth remains <20% in any 6-month period.







Open review Q15

fields.Text:
Isolated left varicocele in young man. Clinical concern?

{{c1::Infertility}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q16. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Isolated left-sided varicoceles are typically idiopathic due to anatomical differences—the left testicular vein drains perpendicular into the left renal vein while the right drains obliquely into the IVC. Isolated right-sided varicocele or new varicocele in older patients should prompt evaluation for secondary causes (such as external compression, thrombus or malignancy).

fields.Text: Varicocele can be clinically important because of {{c1::infertility}}.
fields.Extra:

{{c1::Skin}}

Carotid Doppler velocity criteria shown. Degree of stenosis?

{{c1::70-99%}}
fields.Extra:
Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: IAC Vascular Testing updated carotid stenosis criteria, raising the peak systolic velocity threshold for 50% ICA stenosis from 125 cm/sec to 180 cm/sec due to the original SRU threshold being overly sensitive with poor specificity.

fields.Extra:
Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. The linguine sign reflects the collapsed shell and is seen with intracapsular rupture.

fields.Text: After 10 half-lives, Tl-201 decay-in-storage is roughly {{c1::30-60 days}}.
fields.Extra:
Q20. Correct; difficulty: hard. Tl's short half life (3.05 days) makes rigorous inventory tracking unnecessary.

fields.Text:
Shoulder MRI with biceps tendon medial to the groove. Finding?

{{c1::Medial dislocation of long head of biceps tendon}}
fields.Extra:
Q21. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The long head of the biceps tendon, which normally sits within the biceps groove at the anterior aspect humeral head, is medially dislocated and is immediately anterior to the subscapularis tendon.

fields.Text:
Demyelinating process. Most common early clinical manifestation?

{{c1::Optic neuritis}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q22. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Optic neuritis as a first demyelinating event carries significant long-term risk — approximately 50% of patients presenting with isolated optic neuritis will develop clinically definite MS within 15 years, with MRI white matter lesions at presentation substantially increasing that risk.

{{c1::Osteoarthritis}}

{{c1::Circumscribed}}

Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Circumscribed margins on mammography are well-defined; smooth borders typically associated with benign lesions, contrasting with suspicious irregular or spiculated margins that suggest malignancy.

{{c1::Squamous cell carcinoma}}
fields.Extra:
Q27. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Squamous cell carcinoma is the most common primary malignancy of the paranasal sinus, accounting for approximately 70% of all primary malignancies of the nasopharynx, more than all of the other answer choices combined.

fields.Extra:
Q28. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Upright aerobic exercise (typically treadmill) remains the optimal method of stress testing because it produces the largest increase in cardiac output and coronary blood flow.

fields.Text: In long bones, red-to-yellow marrow conversion begins in the {{c1::epiphysis/apophysis}}.
fields.Extra:
Q29. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The conversion of red to yellow marrow begins peripherally (phalanges) before occurring in the femora/humeri. Within the long bones, the epiphysis is the first to undergo conversion followed by the diaphysis before extending to the metadiaphysis.

fields.Text:
Diffuse gallbladder wall thickening without distension and elevated transaminases. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatitis}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q30. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Acute hepatitis commonly causes diffuse gallbladder wall thickening without gallbladder distension, distinguishing it from acute cholecystitis which typically shows both wall thickening and distension. The sonographic appearance of hepatitis varies but can include "starry sky" appearance.

{{c1::Erosive osteoarthritis}}

fields.Text:
Rapid dementia, akinetic mutism, myoclonus, cortical/striatal diffusion restriction. Etiology?

{{c1::Sporadic CJD}}

1/5
fields.Extra:
Q32. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The classic combination of rapidly progressive dementia, akinetic mutism, and myoclonus with the characteristic MRI pattern of cortical and striatal restricted diffusion strongly indicates sporadic CJD, the most common form representing the majority of prion disease cases in the absence of family history or known exposure risk factors.

fields.Text:
Thyroid uptake only at tongue base with absent normal thyroid bed uptake. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Lingual thyroid}}
fields.Extra:
Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Scintigraphy shows tracer uptake at the tongue base with absent pretracheal uptake indicating that the ectopic tissue is most likely the sole functioning thyroid. This finding is crucial for surgical planning since removal would cause permanent hypothyroidism.

fields.Extra:
Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Medical events require telephone notification to the NRC Operations Center no later than the next calendar day after discovery, followed by a detailed written report within 15 days.

CPA mass with dural-based enhancement and slight IAC extension. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Meningioma}}

fields.Text: CPA meningioma can be dural-based and may slightly extend into the {{c1::internal auditory canal}}.
fields.Extra:
Q43. Correct; difficulty: hard. The contrast-enhancing magnetic resonance image (MRI) shows a homogeneously cerebellar pontine angle (CPA) mass (purple arrow) that slightly extends into the internal auditory canal (IAC) (pink arrow).







Open review Q43

fields.Text:
VCUG in infant with recurrent pyelonephritis. Reflux grades?

{{c1::Left grade 5; right grade 4}}
fields.Extra:
Q44. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Voiding cystourethrogram is usually performed after an initial pediatric urinary tract infection in children under the age of 6 to identify and grade vesicoureteral reflux, which is associated with recurrent urinary tract infections and can result in scarring and renal failure if left untreated.




Open review Q44

fields.Text: VCUG grades vesicoureteral reflux; grade 5 is {{c1::severe dilation/tortuosity with loss of papillary impressions}}.

fields.Text:
Gardner syndrome patient with intra-abdominal mesenteric mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Desmoid tumor}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q45. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Gardner syndrome features facial osteomas and predominantly intra-abdominal mesenteric desmoids, contrasting with sporadic desmoids which are typically extra-abdominal.






Open review Q45

fields.Text: Gardner syndrome is associated with {{c1::intra-abdominal mesenteric desmoid tumors}}.

fields.Text:
Endometrioma with adjacent low-signal structure. Explanation?

{{c1::Endometriotic implant}}

1/3
fields.Extra:
Q46. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The presence of endometriomas on imaging should prompt search for more subtle findings of endometriosis (such as endometriotic implants).




Open review Q46

fields.Text: Endometriomas should prompt search for subtle {{c1::endometriotic implants}}.

fields.Text:
Breast MRI enhancement that is not a mass. BI-RADS term?

{{c1::Non-mass enhancement}}
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Non-mass enhancement (NME) on breast MRI is an enhancing area that is not a mass, may contain interspersed fat, and can extend over variable breast regions. Clustered ring enhancement pattern has the highest malignancy risk, while careful distinction from background parenchymal enhancement is crucial to avoid false-positives.





Open review Q47

fields.Text: Breast MRI non-mass enhancement is enhancement {{c1::not forming a mass and potentially containing interspersed fat}}.
fields.Extra:
Q47. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Non-mass enhancement (NME) on breast MRI is an enhancing area that is not a mass, may contain interspersed fat, and can extend over variable breast regions. Clustered ring enhancement pattern has the highest malignancy risk, while careful distinction from background parenchymal enhancement is crucial to avoid false-positives.

fields.Text:
AIDS patient with pneumothorax, ground-glass opacities, and thin-walled cysts. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Pneumocystis jiroveci pneumonia}}
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: easy. In a patient with AIDS, spontaneous pneumothorax in the setting of bilateral ground-glass opacities and thin walled cysts is highly suggestive of Pneumocystis jiroveci Pneumonia.

fields.Text: PJP in AIDS can cause {{c1::ground-glass opacities, thin-walled cysts, and spontaneous pneumothorax}}.

fields.Text:
Bar phantom with narrow activity slits. What does it test?

{{c1::Gamma camera spatial resolution}}
fields.Extra:
Q49. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Bar phantoms with narrow activity slits are used to measure spatial resolution of gamma cameras, primarily for intrinsic measurements without collimators but also applicable for extrinsic system measurements.

fields.Text: Using fewer MDCT detector rows in the z-axis can increase dose due to {{c1::overbeaming/wasted penumbra dose}}.
fields.Extra:
Q55. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Fewer detectors in the z-axis increase patient dose due to overbeaming—the x-ray beam penumbra extends beyond the detector width, creating "wasted dose" that contributes to patient exposure but not image formation. More detector rows capture a larger fraction of the useful beam, making dose delivery more efficient for equivalent anatomical coverage.




Open review Q55
